subject: Product Management
title: Google Glass
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Google Glass.

hook.text: Google Glass was supposed to be the next smartphone. Instead, wearers were nicknamed 'Glassholes' and banned from bars.

buildup.text: Google launched Glass in 2013 as an 'Explorer Edition' for $1,500. Tech influencers wore it everywhere.

discovery.text: The technology worked. But Google misread the social context — people felt surveilled when someone wore a face camera.

twist.text: Glass solved a problem nobody had. Nobody was desperate to check email while walking or film life hands-free.

climax.text: Google shelved Glass for consumers in 2015. It survived only as a niche enterprise tool for factory workers.

punchline.text: Great technology fails when it ignores social acceptability.

quiz.question: Why did Google Glass fail as a consumer product?
options: It ignored social acceptability — people felt surveilled by face cameras
It was too cheap and felt low-quality
